title: CodexDebateRound
description: Canonical per-round output schema for /codex-debate — structured position exchange between Claude and Codex CLI via --output-schema. Both sides (Claude and Codex) produce one instance of this schema per round. Single source of truth; all references in commands/codex-debate.md and skills/schema-builder/SKILL.md should defer to this file.

position.description: Core position or stance for this round — a concise statement of the debater's current view on the topic.

confidence.description: Confidence in the stated position (0.0 = highly uncertain, 1.0 = fully certain). Decreasing confidence across rounds may signal movement toward consensus.

key_arguments.description: Ordered list of supporting arguments for the stated position. At least one argument is required.

items.description: A single supporting argument or piece of evidence.

agrees_with_opponent.description: Set to true when this debater accepts the opponent's position and is ready to end the debate early (consensus reached). The debate loop checks this flag after every round.

counterpoints.description: Points that directly challenge or rebut the opponent's previous position. May be empty in Round 1 when no prior opponent position exists.

items.description: A single counterpoint targeting an opponent argument.
